content.full_text:
DIRECTORS

VINCENT M. HARDY, DIV. 1

L.B. KOVACS, DIV. 2

KURT O. REITHMAYR, DIV. 3

PATRICK H. MILLER, DIV. 4

CARL E. WARD, DIV. 5

OFFICERS

VINCENT M. HARDY
PRESIDENT

CARL E. WARD
VICE PRESIDENT

L.B. KOVACS
SECRETARY

PATRICK H. MILLER
TREASURER

J.A. HUBERT
ENGINEER MANAGER

CALLEGUAS
MUNICIPAL WATER
DISTRICT

February 13, 1990

Dear Mr. Hallabrin,

I heard your "Board Member's Conflicts of Interests"
presentation at the 1989 ACWA Fall Conference in Monterey,
and would appreciate your advice on an incident.

A District Board Member who has a private Civil
Engineering Practice remarked to the District Manager at
a lunch that he had just stopped by the District office
and dropped off a set of client Plans for signature. The
Manager said he would have the Projects Manager review
them. The Board Member said, "I don't want you to review
them, just sign them."

Upon arrival back to the office, the Projects Manager
had already reviewed the Plans for impact of the Project
on District Facilities and found that a wall would block
access to a District pipeline vault facility. The Board
Member got upset and argued that the wall was only two
feet high and District personnel could climb over it for
vault access. The District did not sign until they checked
with the developer and found out it was a six foot retainer
wall and had the developer agree to change the Plans
providing a stairway access.

Is this a conflict of interest, and if so, what could
or should be done about it, or incidents like this?

Any advice you could give me would be appreciated
very much.

Sincerely,

Vincent M. Hardy

2100 OLSEN ROAD • THOUSAND OAKS, CALIFORNIA 91362
805/526-9323 • 805/529-2752 • FAX: 805/523-5730

I can see this is a mostly blank page with very faint text or markings that are not clearly legible in the image provided. The image appears to be predominantly white/light gray with some faint horizontal lines visible near the bottom.

[illegible]
